role: Portrait.tsx is the mobile-portrait layout shell that composes the top chrome, central legend/wheel stage, scrubber, notice, decisions bar, and detail bottom sheet for the surface interface.
capabilities: Renders the portrait-specific top chrome bar with lens, space, layer, resolution, quantization, centre, live, view, and settings chips
Shows the in-flow decisions bar below the header
Hosts the legend and central wheel-or-state stage as a vertically centred group
Renders the temporal scrubber at the bottom of the stage
Presents selected-point detail as a nonmodal bottom sheet via Disclosure
Renders a Tether indicator tied to the selected sequence
substance_why: The file fully wires a real portrait layout, importing and binding more than a dozen controls and two wheel/detail components to SurfaceState actions.
interactions_present: Pick lens binding via LensChip onPick
Close detail sheet via Disclosure onDismiss
interactions_missing: No gallery UI or trigger is provided even though galleryOpen suppresses the sheet
No keyboard, screen-reader, or focus-management attributes are wired
No drag/gesture handling for the bottom sheet is visible in this file
No orientation or resize handling beyond the static portrait assumption
No loading, error, or empty-state handling beyond WheelOrState
performance_risks: Every SurfaceState change re-renders the entire layout and all chrome chips because no memoization is visible
WheelOrState, Legend, Disclosure, and Tether share the same render cycle, so frequent updates to selected/at/now may cascade
The stamp spread on the header creates a new props object each render, potentially defeating downstream memoization
reuse_verdict_why: The portrait-specific spatial arrangement and control inventory are worth preserving, but the monolithic component should be rebuilt around smaller layout primitives during the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Extract a dedicated PortraitTopBar component for the left/right chip groups
Extract a PortraitStage component unifying Legend and WheelOrState
Extract a BottomSheet host that conditionally renders Disclosure and manages its own open/close state
Separate pure layout chrome from SurfaceState wiring by passing a prepared chrome model object
